steps.title: Identify the form of the equation
steps.content: The equation $d = 45t$ presents distance as a linear function of time. The coefficient 45 is the number multiplying the time variable $t$, which tells us the rate at which distance increases per hour — this is the slope of the linear relationship.

steps.title: Interpret the coefficient using the motion formula
steps.content:
In motion problems, the fundamental relationship is: distance = speed × time.

Our equation $d = 45t$ shows that 45 is the constant multiplying time, so 45 represents the speed.

Since distance is measured in miles and time in hours, this speed must be 45 miles per hour.

That matches choice C: Marisol drove at an average speed of about 45 miles per hour — C is correct.
